quiz.question:
Let's take a look at the pawns and consider pawn islands. 

How many pawn islands does each side have?

answers.text:
White has two pawn islands.

Black has three pawn islands, and two of them consist of isolated pawns.

feedback.text:
Correct! White has the a2-c2 pawn island and the e2-f3 pawn island. 

Black has a pawn island from a7-d7, and then two isolated pawns on f7 and h6 (these count as two distinct islands).

distractors.text: White has one pawn island, and Black has two pawn islands.

feedback.text: Count the pawns that are together and connected, and count those that are isolated too.

distractors.text: White has two pawn islands and Black only has one because the pawns on f7 and h6 don't form an island.

feedback.text: The black pawns on f7 and h6 actually are considered islands. They are isolated pawns.
